key_insight: The machine-learning approach identifies narrower stable composition regions for Sn-based perovskite alloys compared to Pb-based counterparts.
engineering_relevance: This research provides a framework for designing stable hybrid perovskite solar cells through precise compositional engineering.
method: Employs graph neural network interatomic potentials for efficient structure relaxations.
Integrates secondary ML models for direct energy prediction from unrelaxed structures.
Computes free energy landscapes capturing alloy disorder and FA molecular orientations.
evidence_signal: Results indicate maximum stability occurs at high I content, with no stabilization near the center of composition space.
watch_item: The complexity of quaternary alloy modeling may pose challenges in practical applications.
paper_title: Charting the thermodynamic stability of hybrid perovskite alloys with machine learning
